title: HashiCorp Vault unseal keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: HashiCorp Vault unseal key detected. Vault unseal keys (Shamir key shares) are used to reconstruct the root key that decrypts the entire Vault keyring. Exposing them allows attackers to unseal a sealed Vault cluster and gain unrestricted access to every stored secret, credential, certificate, and encryption key in the cluster.
